name: The Bank Of Clovis
description_short: State-chartered FDIC-insured bank founded in 2000, serving Curry County with full-service banking including loans, deposits, and digital services.
description_long:
The Bank of Clovis is a state-chartered bank that was founded on October 16, 2000, and is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank is locally owned and operated by shareholders who are community members including employees, farmers, ranchers, small business owners, construction workers, teachers, doctors, lawyers, nurses, policemen, and firemen from Curry County and surrounding areas. This local ownership structure distinguishes it as a community-focused institution rather than a national chain.

The Bank of Clovis offers a comprehensive suite of traditional banking products and services. These include deposit accounts (checking, savings, money market, CDs, IRAs), payment cards (debit cards, credit cards), and lending products spanning agriculture, business, real estate, auto, personal, and home equity loans. The bank also provides mortgage services for home purchases, refinancing, and new construction. Digital banking capabilities include online banking and mobile banking platforms, with ATM access and full customer support.

What distinguishes The Bank of Clovis is its emphasis on local ownership, community representation, and personalized service. The leadership team is described as "outstanding local people" with education, experience, training, and knowledge in financial services. Their stated commitment is "people taking care of people" and "banking like it should be," positioning themselves as an alternative to larger national institutions by emphasizing personal relationships and community understanding. The low minimum deposit requirement of $50 for their Smartstart Checking account reflects accessibility.

As a community bank, The Bank of Clovis serves customers primarily in and around Curry County. The bank appears well-established and stable with multiple convenient locations and modern digital banking infrastructure. However, as a regional institution, it may have geographic limitations and potentially fewer specialized products than larger national banks. Customers should verify that branch locations and services align with their needs.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and CD Ladder products
Business loans and SBA lending programs
Real estate and mortgage loans (purchase, refinance, new construction)
Online banking and mobile banking platforms
Wealth management and financial planning services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Low minimum deposit requirement ($50) for Smartstart Checking account
Locally owned and operated by community shareholders with local expertise
Full-service banking with comprehensive loan products (agriculture, business, real estate, auto, personal, home equity)
Modern digital banking including online and mobile banking platforms
SBA lending programs for small business support
Mortgage services including home purchase, refinance, and new construction options
Established institution (founded October 16, 2000) with demonstrated stability
cons: Geographic limitation as regional bank—primarily serves Curry County and surrounding areas
Limited information provided about specific interest rates, fees, or competitive product terms on website
May have fewer specialized financial products compared to larger national banks
Website does not clearly display number of branch locations or specific service hours
No information about ATM network size or national/regional ATM partnerships
best_for: Curry County residents and businesses seeking personalized local banking relationships
Small business owners and farmers needing agriculture and SBA lending options
Homebuyers in the region looking for mortgage services from a local lender
Customers who prioritize community banking and personal service over national chains
diagnosis: The Bank of Clovis is best for residents and businesses in Curry County seeking personalized local banking with FDIC protection and a full range of traditional banking services. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional bank serving primarily one county, so customers outside this area or those needing specialized national products may need to explore alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
